purpose: Control how much of the standardized symptom label may appear in synthetic patient utterances.

direct_allowed.description: Common everyday symptom words that patients naturally say directly.

lay_preferred.description: Patients may describe the meaning directly, but the renderer should prefer plain wording over clinical labels.

direct_label_forbidden.description: Clinical or complex labels should not appear as exact patient wording.

style_rules: Use casual spoken Korean, not formal -습니다 style.
For simple symptoms, direct names are realistic and allowed.
For complex clinical labels, describe the experience instead of copying the standard label.
Do not add timing, medication, or agenda content unless the blueprint explicitly asks for it.
For follow-up Q3, express change over time, recurrence, persistence, worsening, improvement, or medication-related course.
